title: Onboarding is seeded by a Glean-bootstrapped AI Context Profile, reviewed and corrected in Work HQ
context: The interview-based wizard (ADR-P6-004) makes the user type their context from scratch — the weakest form of seeding when the org already runs Glean MCP as standard in VS Code. A production-tested bootstrap prompt run through Glean produces a rich 'AI Context Profile': ~30 numbered markdown sections (identity, role scope, programs, team, stakeholders, OKRs, derived work/communication style, an explicit 'How My AI Assistant Should Support Me' guide), with every claim labelled Confirmed / Derived / Unknown-needs-validation. A worked example on a real user validates the quality. The design question: how does this bootstrap integrate with onboarding without violating AD-4 (no MCP is ever load-bearing) and AD-5 (the profile contains personnel-adjacent data — manager, reports, start dates — that must never reach the committed repo)?

options.label: A
options.summary: Status quo: manual interview phases only; users with Glean paste findings in by hand
pros: No new UI, no parser, no new file contract — ships exactly what ADR-P6-004 drafted
Zero MCP coupling; AD-4 trivially satisfied
cons: Throws away the highest-leverage seeding available: minutes of Glean research versus a shallow five-field form
Typed-from-scratch context is thin, unsourced, and skips exactly the derived work-style insight the worked example proves is extractable
The user has already production-tested the bootstrap; not designing for it guarantees an unofficial workflow grows outside the wizard

options.label: B
options.summary: Optional bootstrap path: the wizard hands the assistant the bootstrap prompt, the assistant writes context/profile.md via Glean, Work HQ renders every section as an editable card, and a corrections handoff locks the profile in — manual interview remains the first-class fallback
pros: Best of both: enterprise-grounded depth when Glean is present, unchanged manual path when it is not — AD-4 holds because the bootstrap is a path choice, never a requirement

The review-and-correct phase turns AI research into human-verified context: per-section editing with Confirmed/Derived/Unknown status visible, and only edited sections travel back in the corrections handoff

One durable artefact (gitignored context/profile.md) becomes the deep Tier-2 context file; me.md/org.md/active.md are distilled from it, so the interview phases become confirmation rather than data entry
Reuses existing Work HQ machinery: the handoff dock carries the bootstrap prompt out and the corrections back — no new interaction pattern
cons: Real work: a markdown section parser, an editable-sections review UI, two new handoff kinds, and a profile file contract
The bootstrap prompt becomes a committed, versioned artefact that must be kept assistant-agnostic and org-portable
Two onboarding paths to test and keep coherent (seeded vs manual) — the test matrix grows

options.label: C
options.summary: Fully agent-driven: the assistant runs the bootstrap AND the corrections conversationally in chat; no review UI in Work HQ
pros: No new Work HQ screens — the os-helper Butler Protocol absorbs the bootstrap as another interview source
cons: Reviewing ~30 sections conversationally is exactly the clunky, low-visibility experience the visual wizard exists to replace
No per-section status display (Confirmed/Derived/Unknown), no side-by-side editing — corrections become a wall of chat text
Contradicts the product direction: the user explicitly wants editable sections in the onboarding page with a paste-back correction loop

decision: Adopt Option B. The wizard's seeding phase offers two paths. BOOTSTRAP PATH: Work HQ shows the bootstrap prompt (a committed template at templates/bootstrap-profile-prompt.md with the user's name filled in) with Collect & Copy ('## Work HQ handoff · profile-bootstrap · <date>'); the user pastes it into their assistant, which researches via Glean MCP and writes the AI Context Profile to context/profile.md (gitignored, durable-personal — it contains personnel-adjacent data and must never be committed, per AD-5). Work HQ detects the file, parses it generically on '## <heading>' boundaries, and renders each section as an editable card with its Confirmed/Derived/Unknown status chip and an edited badge. The user corrects any section; Collect & Copy emits '## Work HQ handoff · profile-corrections · <date>' carrying ONLY the edited sections plus the lock-in ask: apply corrections to context/profile.md, then distil me.md, org.md (through the sensitivity gate — no personnel data in the committed file), and active.md seeds from it. MANUAL PATH: the ADR-P6-004 interview phases, unchanged, for users without Glean or who prefer typing. Both paths converge on the Right-now and Finish phases. The wizard phase order changes to make this possible: Wire-your-assistant moves BEFORE seeding, because the bootstrap requires a working assistant bridge. context/profile.md becomes a first-class Tier-2 durable-personal file with a routing-table row ('load before drafting or personalising, after me.md') — an AGENTS.md edit made by the implementing agent, never by the wizard. The production-tested prompt text (supplied via OQ-005, resolved) lives at templates/bootstrap-profile-prompt.md. Its structural contract is binding: title header '# <Name> — AI Context Profile', '## <Title>' sections (numbered or unnumbered — the parser splits on heading boundaries and must not assume numbering), per-claim Confirmed/Derived/Unknown labels, an 'Open questions / assumptions to validate' section, and a closing 'How my AI assistant should support me' section. The template also carries the production follow-up prompt (first-person conversion), applied at lock-in.
consequences: ADR-P6-004's phase list is amended (same ADR, still Proposed): Orientation → Wire your assistant → Seed your profile (path choice) → Review & correct (seeded: section cards; manual: interview forms) → Right now → Finish. New file contract entries in docs/specs/file-contracts.md: context/profile.md structure and the profile-bootstrap / profile-corrections handoff kinds. The Glean dependency is confined to the assistant side — Work HQ itself never calls MCP, so AD-4 is preserved structurally. Demo/mock data for the review UI must be a FICTIONAL persona; the real worked example (example-profile.md) stays untracked and is never committed. os-helper gains the lock-in contract (apply corrections, distil, confirm). Stories 002/003/004/006 and the test plan need revision; two new stories cover the bootstrap phase and the review UI.
costCapacity: No recurring cost to Work HQ (local only, no MCP calls from the app). The bootstrap itself costs one burst of Glean MCP queries per onboarding run — one-time per user, within normal enterprise Glean usage; the profile is thereafter read locally per the consolidate-once pattern (AD-4).
